blocks:
Ayahs

ٱلْقَارِعَةُ (۱)
The Catastrophe! (1)
مَا ٱلْقَارِعَةُ (۲)
What is the Catastrophe? (2)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلْقَارِعَةُ (۳)
What will show you what is the Catastrophe? (3)
يَوْمَ يَكُونُ ٱلنَّاسُ كَٱلْفَرَاشِ ٱلْمَبْثُوثِ (٤)
[It is] the day when mankind will be like scattered moths (4)
وَتَكُونُ ٱلْجِبَالُ كَٱلْعِهْنِ ٱلْمَنفُوشِ (٥)
and the mountains like carded wool. (5)
فَأَمَّا مَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ (٦)
As for him, whose deeds weigh heavy in the scales, (6)
فَهُوَ فِى عِيشَةٍ رَّاضِيَةٍ (۷)
he will have a pleasing life. (7)
وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ (۸)
But as for him, whose deeds weigh light in the scales, (8)
فَأُمُّهُۥ هَاوِيَةٌ (۹)
his home will be the Abyss. (9)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا هِيَهْ (۱٠)
And what will show you what it is? (10)
نَارٌ حَامِيَةٌۢ (۱۱)
It is a scorching fire! (11)

Key Points

Al-Qāri'ah (The Striking Calamity) comes from qar', meaning the striking of one thing upon another. One of the names of the Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah) is Al-Qāri'ah, because it begins with a striking cry and its punishment is striking (or overwhelming). We read in narrations that Imam Ali عليه السلام and his infallible children are the Mīzān (Scale) and the criterion for weighing the deeds of humans on the Day of Judgment.(125) Perhaps the reason the word Mawāzīn (Scales) is used in the plural is that the various deeds of humans are weighed by various means, and every deed has its own specific scale (Mīzān). Mawāzīn is the plural of Mīzān, meaning the instrument of measurement. It is clear that human deeds are not material things possessing weight and mass to be measured by ordinary scales; rather, every category of human deeds has its own specific criterion for measurement. A mother is a shelter and refuge for all her children, but there (in the Hereafter), the mother and refuge for a group of humans is Hellfire (Dūzakh). Hāwiyah (The Abyss) comes from hawā, meaning falling or plunging, and Hellfire is the place where a group will fall. Ḥāmiyah (Fiercely Hot) comes from ḥamā, meaning intensity of heat. The colorful mountains, as a result of being fragmented and reduced to dust, will become like colored carded wool. Kal-'ihnil-manfūsh (Like carded wool). Manfūsh comes from nafsh, meaning the opening and pulling apart of wool until its fibers are separated.(126) Lightness and heaviness are not exclusive to material things. Thus, meaningless words are called "light speech." Perhaps the meaning of thaqulat mawāzīnuh (whose scales are heavy) and khaffat mawāzīnuh (whose scales are light) refers to the value and quality of the deed, or perhaps it refers to its scarcity or abundance. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The meaning of the lightness of the deed is the scarcity of the deed, and the meaning of the heaviness of the deed is its abundance. Qillatul-ḥisāb wa kathratuh (The scarcity and abundance of the reckoning).(127) A person once belittled Salman (al-Fārsī), asking, "Who and what are you?" He replied: "The beginning of you and me is a drop of semen, and the end of you and me is a corpse. This is the world, but on the Day of Judgment, مَن ثقلت موازینه فهو الکریم و من خفّت موازینه فهو لئیم (He whose scales are heavy, they are the honored ones; and he whose scales are light, they are the base ones).(128) Whoever's scale is heavy will be honored, and whoever's scale is light will be humiliated and blamed.

125) Biḥār al-Anwār, Vol. 7, p. 252. 126) Tafsīr Rāhnamā. 127) Iḥtijāj Ṭabarsī, Vol. 1, p. 363. Quoted from Rāhnamā. 128) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the striker of the arrogant and the striking down of haughty spirits. «القارعة ما القارعة»

2- The Day of Judgment is beyond human comprehension. Even the Prophet does not know about it without divine revelation. «و ما ادراك ما القارعة»

3- The Day of Judgment is a day of bewilderment and confusion for humanity. «كالفراش المبثوث»

4- Paradise is granted for a price (deeds), not for excuses. «من ثقلت موازینه فهو فى عیشة راضیة»

5- A life full of complete joy is exclusive to the Hereafter, because in this world, alongside achievements, there are concerns about illness, theft, envy, loss, and transience. «عیشة راضیة»

6- Satisfaction with life is among the signs of the heavenly society. «فهو فى عیشة راضیة»

7- The basis of reward and punishment is action, which is weighed by the scale of justice. «ثقلت موازینه... خفّت موازینه»
